source.name: PowerShell about_Quoting_Rules
source.license_notes: Summarized guidance with source link; do not copy full docs.

goal: Run Windows local commands with safe quoting, literal paths, and shell-aware path handling.
steps: Use PowerShell -NoProfile to avoid startup hook noise when needed.
Use single quotes for literal PowerShell strings; double quotes when variable expansion is needed.
In Bash/Git Bash use forward slashes such as E:/DataBase/..., not raw E:\DataBase\...
Quote paths with spaces and avoid composing destructive commands across shells.
verification: No conda GBK startup noise when -NoProfile is used
safety_notes: Use native PowerShell file cmdlets for Windows file operations.
Avoid destructive commands unless explicitly authorized.

quality.notes: PowerShell quoting rules plus observed local Bash path issue.

retrieval.ai_summary: On Windows, use PowerShell -NoProfile and -LiteralPath for exact paths; in Bash use forward slashes. Avoid raw backslash Windows paths in Bash and report corrected commands.
